title: centranthus angustifolius subsp. battandieri
intro: Centranthus angustifolius subsp. battandieri, also known as the Narrow-leaved Valerian, is a perennial plant species in the valerian family, Valerianaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and sesquiterpenes. The Narrow-leaved Valerian has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, promoting sleep, and relieving menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. The plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
